answer:
The most likely correct answer is **\boxed{G}**, an attempt to discredit the Pope's authority in England.

This Act of Supremacy (1534) broke England's ties with the Catholic Church by declaring Henry VIII the supreme head of the Church of England, effectively undermining papal authority. It was part of Henry’s broader effort to assert royal control over religious matters, particularly after his break with Rome over the annulment of his marriage to Catherine of Aragon. The passage explicitly rejects foreign (papal) authority in favor of royal supremacy.
